semantic_description: Verifies an in-place array reversal function. The input contract owns an integer array a of length n with contents l and requires 0 <= n and Zlength(l) == n. The function uses a while loop with two indices, left starting at 0 and right starting at n - 1, swapping symmetric elements until left >= right. The postcondition requires the same array memory to contain rev(l). Empty, singleton, and already-crossed pointer cases skip the loop and must still satisfy rev(l) == l for those lengths. The function mutates the input array in place and returns no scalar value.
